meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to use SaaS pricing models to overcome the 'no project ideas' block. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for fast delivery.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, reverse-engineer high-converting SaaS pricing pages. By analyzing successful pricing tiers and feature gates, you uncover the core value proposition of profitable micro-SaaS projects. Use Antigravity to scaffold the structure and Claude Code to iterate on the copy and logic instantly.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a freelancer's graveyard. You have the technical stack, the ambition, and the billable hours, but you are paralyzed by the lack of a 'million-dollar idea.' You are looking for a lightning bolt, but what you really need is a map of where the money is already flowing.

SaaS pricing pages are not just lists of features; they are blueprints of solved problems. When you deconstruct how top-tier products price their services, you stop guessing what users need and start building exactly what they are already paying for. Let's turn market data into your next project.

steps.title: Identify Target Market via Pricing
steps.description: Find a niche SaaS competitor. Analyze their pricing tiers to understand the specific pain points they solve for different user segments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scrape the pricing page of [URL] and extract all feature lists, pricing tiers, and the 'best for' descriptions into a structured JSON format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the extracted JSON and identify the top 3 underserved pain points based on the feature gaps in the lower pricing tiers.'

steps.title: Define Your MVP Value Proposition
steps.description: Create a simplified pricing model that targets the lowest tier of an existing competitor but adds one high-value automated feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a landing page copy focused on a 'Starter' tier that solves [Pain Point] with an automated AI-driven workflow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a pricing.tsx component using Tailwind CSS that highlights a clear competitive advantage over [Competitor Name].'

steps.title: Build the Pricing Logic Engine
steps.description: Implement the backend logic to handle subscription tiers using Stripe or Lemonsqueezy to ensure your project is immediately monetizable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe checkout integration boilerplate for a Next.js application with three tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement webhooks to handle subscription upgrades and downgrades using the Stripe API.'

steps.title: Simulate User Conversion Flow
steps.description: Use Claude Code to simulate how a user moves from landing page to checkout, identifying friction points in your pricing structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a user journey map for a SaaS product where the primary conversion goal is the 'Pro' tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Simulate a user flow from landing to checkout; identify any UI bottlenecks in the pricing selection component.'

steps.title: Final Deployment and Launch
steps.description: Package your project for deployment, ensuring your pricing page is responsive and the checkout flow is fully operational.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment-ready configuration file for Vercel, ensuring all environment variables for Stripe are properly mapped.
steps.claude_code_command: claude deploy 'Push the final build to production and run a smoke test on the checkout callback URL.'

pro_tips: Always include a 'Contact Sales' button for your highest tier to capture enterprise leads, even if you don't have a sales team yet.
Use 'Price Anchoring' by making your middle tier the most visually prominent, as this is where 60-70% of your conversions will occur.
Never hide your pricing. If a customer has to email you to get a quote, they have already moved on to a competitor.
Offer an annual discount of 20% to boost your immediate cash flow, which is vital for early-stage SaaS sustainability.
A/B test your pricing labels (e.g., 'Starter' vs. 'Early Adopter')—the psychology of the label often outweighs the price point itself.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing page is live, focus on building one high-value feature that justifies the 'Pro' tier price. Use your analytics to track which tier visitors click most, then iterate on the copy to increase your conversion rate.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation and the market insight to stop hunting for ideas and start shipping profitable products. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
